meaning: n.(足球)开球,(社会集会等)开始
examples: During the kickoff, the team structures were discussed and mapped out for the next release.
This effort started with the kickoff agile workshop, using Rational Team Concert as the tool of choice.
Zenani Mandela died early on Friday morning, two days after her thirteenth birthday, while being driven home from the World Cup kickoff concert.
After 562 runs, receptions and kickoff returns in his four-year NFL career, he still hasn't fumbled.
Apparently I'll learn to love the other one--it's going to be the network's promotional kickoff song.
